role: Pentagon Chief of Staff for Asst. Secretary of Defense for Special Operations
bio: Ariane Tabatabai is a German-Iranian political scientist who served as chief of staff to the Assistant Secretary of Defense for Special Operations and Low-Intensity Conflict at the Pentagon. She was recruited in 2014 by [Saeed Khatibzadeh](/people/saeed-khatibzadeh) as a founding member of the [Iran Experts Initiative](/events/iei-email-leak-2023), a covert [Iranian Foreign Ministry](/orgs/ministry-of-foreign-affairs) influence operation coordinated through [IPIS](/orgs/ipis). Leaked emails showed she consulted IPIS head [Mostafa Zahrani](/people/mostafa-zahrani) about conference attendance — including an Israeli conference invitation — and informed him of opportunities to testify before Congress. In February 2021, [Robert Malley](/people/robert-malley) hired her to join his Iran team at the State Department, and she subsequently moved to the Pentagon. After the [IEI leak](/events/iei-email-leak-2023) in September 2023, the Pentagon launched a security review of her clearance; she was ultimately retained and her top-secret SCI clearance maintained.
editorial_notes: Core IEI member. Pentagon review concluded she was 'thoroughly and properly vetted.'
